growth_records.strain: soda-lake isolates (>100 strains; e.g., K90mix, ALE20)
growth_records.medium: Sodium carbonate/bicarbonate soda medium (pH ~10), thiosulfate (batch) / sulfide (chemostat)

up.method: derived from mu_max and growth yield

growth_records.cite: Dimitry Y. Sorokin et al. The Microbial Sulfur Cycle at Extremely Haloalkaline Conditions of Soda Lakes. Frontiers in Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2011.00044. [PMC3128939]
growth_records.snippet: Maximal specific growth rate 0.25 h-1 ... Maximal growth yield with S2O3(2-) 6.5 mg protein mmol-1 ... pH optimum 10.0-10.2 ... Upper salt limit 4.3 M Na+.
growth_records.notes: Review Table 2. mu_max=0.25 h-1; td=ln2/0.25=2.77 h. Yield 6.5 mg protein/mmol thiosulfate (highest of the four genera). Derived qS = 0.25/6.5 = 0.0385 mmol/(mg protein.h) = 38.5 mmol/(g protein.h) per g protein. pH midpoint 10.1 of optimum 10.0-10.2; temperature not stated. Chemostat noted for sulfide-limited growth; batch for thiosulfate. Review-aggregated values.
